mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment captured by month-over-month increases in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income that drives aggregate consumer spending and lifts S&P 500 earnings expectations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are negative in more than 50% of months over a 2-year rolling window, or the strategy Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0%, diminishing labor slack accelerates wage growth and consumption, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered months over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously accelerating consumer prices alongside a federal funds rate anchored near zero erodes real policy rates and pushes investors into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the joint signal Rank IC is below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a surge in manufacturing momentum that lifts cyclical corporate earnings and drives positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In months when INDPRO rises more than 0.5%, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly increases in housing starts reflect strengthening construction activity and durable goods demand that boost cyclical equity exposure, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and payroll employment confirm a broad-based demand recovery, amplifying positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days beyond either single-series signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a 20-day Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term does not significantly outperform the sum of individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% marks the end of emergency monetary accommodation, raising the discount rate applied to future earnings and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month jump in consumer prices exceeding 0.4% signals acute inflation pressure that compresses real corporate margins and triggers negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% in a month, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year window.
